title: Koko Eating Bananas
prompt: Koko has piles of bananas with piles[i] bananas in the i-th pile. She eats k bananas per hour, choosing one pile each hour. If a pile has fewer than k bananas, she finishes it and waits the rest of that hour. Return the minimum integer k that lets her finish all piles within h hours.
hints: Higher k → fewer hours. Lower k → more hours. The relationship is monotonic.
Binary search over k in [1, max(piles)].
Hours used at speed k = sum(ceil(p/k)).

optimal.approach: Binary search the answer; check feasibility in O(n).

pitfalls: Integer overflow in other languages; in JS, ceil division must round up correctly.
Wrong upper bound (must be max(piles), not sum).
followups: Capacity to ship within D days (LC 1011).
Split array into m subarrays minimizing max sum.
